attempts.hypothesis: The counter state update and wraparound are correct, but p_count_o should continuously expose count_q instead of being gated to zero when sw_req_i is low.
attempts.failure_summary: Both cocotb tests failed. Overflow test still observed p_count_o == 0 when 1 was expected after overflow.

attempts.hypothesis: sw_req_i is an event source rather than a clear/read request, so the counter should increment on sw_req_i || cpu_trig_i, expose count_q continuously, and clear only on reset.
attempts.failure_summary: Both cocotb tests failed. Overflow test still observed p_count_o == 0 when 1 was expected after overflow.

attempts.hypothesis: The testbench expects asynchronous active-low reset. Keep the original software-read/cpu-trigger behavior and change only reset polarity.
attempts.failure_summary: Both cocotb tests failed. Overflow test still observed p_count_o == 0 when 1 was expected after overflow.

attempts.hypothesis: The expected behavior combines active-low reset, continuous count visibility, and increment-on-either-input event semantics with natural wrapping overflow.
